artworks.title: Morning in Trogir
artworks.description: This painting is about the city of Trogir in Croatia. We went there on a yachting trip, and in the morning I went out to look for a pharmacy to buy some shampoo I had forgotten at home. I saw a beautiful view of the city after the rain, still morning-quiet and not yet awake. That tranquility enchanted me, and I decided to paint it.

artworks.title: Sky Above the Quiet Land
artworks.description: Painted en plein air in a small city Gaal, this oil painting captures the quiet majesty of foothills. Rolling green hills unfold beneath a luminous sky, with layers of clouds shifting in soft pastels and sunlit whites. The scene, painted from life, reflects the tranquil harmony of the landscape, where distant mountains meet open fields and time seems to slow. A gentle tribute to the poetry of nature as seen through the artist’s eyes.

artworks.title: Dont push the horses
artworks.description: A group of wild horses stands on a cliff, overlooking the deep blue ocean. The strong contrast between the golden tones of the land and the rich blue of the sea reflects my deep love for these colors—yellow and blue—the colors of my homeland, Ukraine. This painting is about freedom, strength, and connection to nature. It brings a sense of peace and movement into any space.

artworks.title: Winter Glow
artworks.description:
In this oil painting, I capture the quiet beauty of a winter morning. Soft golden sunlight touches the snow-covered trees and house, contrasting with cool blue shadows in the drifting snow.

A solitary figure walking through the landscape adds a sense of depth, scale, and peaceful contemplation. I focused on light, atmosphere, and delicate color transitions to express the tenderness and serenity of winter.

Oil on canvas. Original artwork

artworks.title: Toward the Mountains
artworks.description:
This painting was inspired by a winter carriage ride with horses in a park near Filzmoos. As we moved slowly along the snowy road, the landscape opened toward the distant alpine mountains, and the quiet rhythm of the horses created a peaceful sense of travel through the winter scenery.

In this work I wanted the viewer to feel as if they were sitting in the carriage, looking forward between the horses as the path leads deeper into the valley. The warm tones of the horses contrast with the cool light of snow and mountains, capturing the crisp air, soft winter colors, and the calm atmosphere of an alpine day.

The scene reflects a moment of simple beauty — a traditional horse-drawn ride through a snowy landscape where nature, movement, and silence come together.

artworks.title: Where Time Rests
artworks.description:
This painting was inspired by the peaceful canals of Laxenburg Park, near Vienna — a place once cherished by Empress Elisabeth of Austria (Sisi). I was fascinated by the play of light and shadow on the calm water surface, where reflections of trees blend with drifting water plants, creating a dreamy, timeless atmosphere.

In this work, I sought to capture not only the beauty of nature but also the quiet elegance and nostalgia that still linger in the park where Sisi once found moments of rest and contemplation.

artworks.title: Fanningberg 3
artworks.description:
One of six works created during my plein air session in Fanningberg

I painted this work outdoors in Fanningberg, Austria, surrounded by the quiet vastness of the Alps. The winter landscape was almost monochrome, yet full of subtle tonal shifts — soft greys, muted greens, and countless variations of white that carried the feeling of cold air and deep stillness.

artworks.title: Sea light and silence
artworks.description:
I painted this piece from life during a plein air session in Alanya, Turkey, at sunset. In that quiet evening, the sky and sea seemed to dissolve into one another, softened by a calm, muted light.

A warm glow briefly breaks through the clouds, capturing a fleeting moment of silence, light, and stillness. Alongside that, it holds the wonderful mood of painting en plein air with the other girls.

artworks.title: Snowdrop Symphony
artworks.description: A soft blanket of snowdrops stretches along a garden path, guiding the eye toward a quiet country house. The trees are still bare, but the air is full of promise — the first light of spring gently warms the earth, awakening color and life after a long winter. This painting captures the delicate transition between seasons, filled with freshness, hope, and a quiet sense of renewal.

artworks.title: The Silent Encounter
artworks.description: In this painting, I explore the quiet presence of nature in winter. A white hare stands still within a snow-covered forest, surrounded by soft light and muted colors. I use gentle blues, violets, and warm highlights to convey a sense of calm, silence, and attentive stillness. This work reflects a moment of quiet connection between the viewer and the natural world.

artworks.title: Sunset in the haven
artworks.description: A stunning sunset reflects off the calm waters of a marina Pirovac in Croatia, casting a warm glow on the sailboats gently moored. I captured the quiet elegance of the evening, blending soft hues of orange, pink, and blue to evoke a sense of tranquility and stillness.

artworks.title: Mediterranean Mood
artworks.description: A luminous still life inspired by the warmth and charm of the Mediterranean. Two vibrant lemons rest on a richly patterned tablecloth, set against an ornamental tile motif. The composition is executed on transparent-primed linen, with selected areas intentionally left unpainted to accentuate the natural texture and character of the canvas. This interplay between vivid color and raw surface enhances the authenticity and visual depth of the work.

artworks.title: Fanningberg 6
artworks.description:
This painting is part of my alpine plein air series, created during my time in the Austrian mountains. I worked directly from nature, capturing the shifting atmosphere of a winter day — the cool air, distant light, and the quiet rhythm of snow-covered hills.

I focused on broad, expressive brushstrokes and layered oil paint to convey movement within stillness. The mountains dissolve into soft blue and violet tones, while the foreground snow reflects subtle variations of light. The trees and distant houses appear almost like silhouettes, emphasizing space and depth.

For me, this work is about the feeling of winter calm — not frozen, but alive with color and air. It invites the viewer to pause and breathe in the vastness of the landscape.

artworks.title: Lazy Hours
artworks.description:
This scene was painted from the view of our apartment in Croatia, in the soft stillness of the afternoon. The morning rush is over — towels lie on sunbeds, and guests quietly enjoy the sun, the pool, and the scent of cypress trees.

The air is warm, the hills glow in the background, and time seems to slow down. I wanted to capture that gentle, sleepy moment when the day is already full — and yet not quite over.

artworks.title: Spring in Vienna
artworks.description: This enchanting landscape captures the beauty of a blossoming orchard bathed in warm sunlight. Lush green grass stretches beneath delicate trees adorned with vibrant flowers, inviting viewers into a serene spring day. The bright blue sky enhances the sense of tranquility, making this piece a perfect addition for those who appreciate nature's beauty and seek to bring a touch of peaceful elegance into their space. I like my weekend with frends in Vienna park and memorised it in the picture.

artworks.title: Finally, a rest
artworks.description:
This scene was created by me in January 2022 during a skiing trip to Austria. Those were wonderful days spent with friends, enjoying life, peace, and tranquility. There was also that feeling, when, exhausted from the wonderful physical fatigue, you finally see the goal, where you can rest. After the vacation, I decided to combine my long-time passion for painting with learning German by enrolling in a painting course conducted in German. During the course, I was invited to participate in a competition. I chose this painting as my entry, and it won.

This marked the beginning of my journey into the world of painting, inspiring me to explore more deeply and experiment with oil paints instead of acrylics. This piece is particularly special to me—it represents joy, relaxation, and the beginning of something new.

artworks.title: Fanningberg 4
artworks.description:
One of six works created during my plein air session in Fanningberg

I painted this work outdoors in Fanningberg, Austria, surrounded by the quiet vastness of the Alps. The winter landscape was almost monochrome, yet full of subtle tonal shifts — soft greys, muted greens, and countless variations of white that carried the feeling of cold air and deep stillness.

artworks.title: Mediterranean Morning Light
artworks.description: At dawn, while the harbor still slept, I painted this view from the deck of a catamaran. Quiet boats rested on the water, a stone house with a red roof glowed in the first sunlight, and the sky promised a new Mediterranean day. This work carries the stillness and beauty of a Croatian morning by the sea.

artworks.title: Two Shadows on the Snow
artworks.description:
This gouache painting was created from life in the Salzburg region and captures a quiet, personal moment — a winter walk with my son. Our elongated blue shadows stretch across the snowy road, becoming a key emotional element of the composition.

I was drawn to the clarity of the cold winter day: the deep blue sky, the snow-covered mountains in the distance, and the pine trees lining the path. The figures are intentionally simplified, allowing the focus to remain on light, space, and the feeling of togetherness. Working in gouache helped me preserve the softness of the snow and the immediacy of painting directly from life.

artworks.title: Two Skis
artworks.description:
I painted this gouache from life, capturing a quiet moment on a winter chairlift. From my seat, I looked down at my skis suspended above fresh snow as the lift moved slowly forward through frosted trees.

Working directly from observation, I focused on the feeling of gentle motion, cold air, and muted winter light. This painting reflects a brief pause between movement and stillness — the calm, intimate moment of being lifted through the winter landscape.

artworks.title: Chanel №5
artworks.description:
This still life captures the timeless elegance of pink roses arranged in a rustic brown vase, set against a deep green background that enhances the vividness of the flowers. I have bought this roses on Nashmarket in Vienna. They were fresh and wild. I took presents from my husband - Chanel №5 and pearl necklace, and fell in love with with beaty during my drawing.
Perfect for anyone who appreciates classic beauty and sophistication, this piece would be a stunning addition to a living room or bedroom. It’s also a wonderful gift for art lovers and those with an eye for elegance in decor and smell of Chanel 5.

artworks.title: Vienna's view
artworks.description: This Viennas cityscape showcases a charming blend of architecture with striking church spires rising above colorful rooftops. I was happy to see it from my studio in 9.district Vienna. Perfect for art lovers who appreciate urban landscapes, this piece invites viewers to explore the beauty of city life and its rich history.

artworks.title: Unbroken Bloom
artworks.description:
I painted this piece as a quiet reflection on resilience. These small yellow flowers appeared on a grey stretch of asphalt, where life seems least expected. I was struck by how confidently they reached for the light, transforming an ordinary, muted surface into something alive and radiant.

Through this work, I wanted to capture that fragile yet powerful moment when spring begins to return. Even in the most uninviting places, beauty insists on existing. For me, these blossoms are a symbol of inner strength and renewal, a reminder that no matter how grey the surroundings may feel, life continues to grow, softly but persistently, from within.

artworks.title: Golden Vineyards of Vienna
artworks.description:
I created this painting inspired by the golden vineyards of Vienna in October. The rows of sunlit vines lead toward the city, softly fading into the distance under a dramatic autumn sky. I wanted to capture the warm glow of the season and the peaceful rhythm of the landscape as it prepares for winter.
This piece brings the atmosphere of Vienna’s wine hills—bright, calm, and full of autumn light—into any interior.

artworks.title: Viennese Café Afternoon
artworks.description: Painted en plein air, this work captures the warm and inviting atmosphere of a popular café in Vienna, located not far from where I live. The play of sunlight and shadows on the pavement, the gentle rustle of leaves, and the relaxed conversations of locals enjoying their drinks reflect the city’s unique charm. It is a glimpse into everyday Viennese life — peaceful, lively, and full of character.

artworks.title: Spring Awakening
artworks.description:
This vibrant oil painting captures the essence of early spring with a delicate bouquet of yellow tulips, white flowers, and sprigs of mimosa, arranged in a transparent glass vase. The rich contrast between the dark background and the bright flowers highlights their freshness and liveliness. A small porcelain rabbit figurine at the base adds a charming touch, symbolizing renewal and the awakening of nature.

The expressive brushstrokes and interplay of light and shadow create a dynamic yet harmonious composition. Perfect for bringing warmth and elegance to any interior, this artwork is ideal for those who appreciate floral themes and the beauty of seasonal change.

artworks.title: Lavender and Clay
artworks.description:
This still life carries a special story. A friend of mine was visiting Cyprus and sent me photos from his trip. One picture showed a beautiful, traditional wine jug. I told him how striking it was and how inspiring it would be to paint it. Then, something wonderful happened—he received the jug as a gift from a local restaurant and brought it back for me!

In this painting, I placed the Cypriot clay jug next to a jar of freshly picked lavender and a single chili pepper. The warm tones, soft window light, and simple arrangement reflect both the Mediterranean charm of the object and the joy of unexpected gifts shared between friends.

artworks.title: Magnolia in bloom
artworks.description:
I painted this work en plein air in Setagaya Park in Vienna, during the peak of magnolia bloom. The expressive branches and vibrant pink blossoms stood out beautifully against the soft spring sky.

I focused on capturing the light, air, and shifting colors of the petals, which move from deep rose to delicate blush. This painting reflects a fleeting spring moment — full of freshness, lightness, and quiet joy.

artworks.title: Green Companions
artworks.description: I painted this piece over a long period, exploring a layered painting technique. It captures a special moment in May in Austria when my son and I encountered sheep in lush green fields. One mother sheep and her lamb looked at us with curiosity, as if asking, “How are you? Where are you headed?” I aimed to express that curiosity in this layered composition.

artworks.title: Peonies on the Balcony
artworks.description: These peonies were originally bought to paint a small wedding card from life — just a quick gouache sketch. But their delicate beauty and the way the sunlight danced on the petals were impossible to resist. I ended up creating not only the card, but also this oil painting. Captured on a quiet afternoon, this piece reflects the joy and spontaneity of working from nature. The soft pink blossoms, vivid shadows, and gentle light together tell a story of fleeting inspiration turned into something lasting.

artworks.title: Fanningberg 2
artworks.description:
This painting is part of a series of six plein air works created during a skiing trip in Fanningberg (Austria). Despite the winter conditions, I painted directly from nature, returning each time to the same spot in the forest. Although the viewpoint remained unchanged, the atmosphere and the state of nature were constantly transforming.

Throughout the day, wet snow was falling, wrapping the forest in a shifting veil of mist. The trees would appear clearly for a moment and then dissolve again into a soft grey haze. This constant movement of air, moisture, and light created an endlessly changing mood.

Using oil paints, I focused on capturing not just the visual landscape, but the emotional state of the moment — the quiet, the cold humidity in the air, the softness of the snow, and the fragile presence of the trees emerging and disappearing in the fog.

It was an intense and fascinating experience to observe how the same place could transform so dramatically within hour

artworks.title: Purple and Grey
artworks.description: This painting captures a personal and tender moment. On my birthday, my husband gave me a bouquet of flowers—but before he did, he asked me to choose the ones I would most like to paint. I chose these graceful bellflowers for their rich purples and soft whites, drawn to their elegant forms and the beautiful way they cast shadows in the afternoon light. This artwork is a celebration of love, thoughtfulness, and the quiet beauty found in everyday moments.

artworks.title: Fanningberg 1
artworks.description:
This painting is part of a series of six plein air works created during a skiing trip. Despite the winter conditions, I painted directly from nature, returning each time to the same spot in the forest. Although the viewpoint remained unchanged, the atmosphere and the state of nature were constantly transforming.

Snow, skiing, fresh mountain air, and the quiet beauty of the Alps are reflected through emotion, movement, and expressive brushstrokes. This work is about the feeling of freedom, calm, and joy that only the Austrian winter landscape can give.

artworks.title: Silent Majesty
artworks.description:
This oil painting captures the serene beauty of a winter landscape bathed in crisp, clear light. Towering snow-covered fir trees stand against a deep blue sky, their branches heavy with fresh snow. The untouched snowy path invites the viewer into a tranquil and peaceful world, evoking a sense of stillness and harmony with nature. The textured brushstrokes bring depth and movement to the scene, making it a perfect piece for lovers of winter’s quiet charm.

This artwork was painted in Austria during a skiing trip. The breathtaking view from the ski trail inspired a sense of peace, freedom, and mystery. The treetops, bending as if alive, seem to beckon the viewer into the enchanting snowy world.

artworks.title: Delicate Solitude
artworks.description: Experience “Delicate solitude,” a captivating oil on canvas that celebrates the tender solitude of nature. This artwork showcases a solitary orange poppy in full bloom, its vibrant presence imbued with a gentle loneliness that speaks to the quiet moments of reflection in life. Set against an elegantly patterned vase, two partially drooping buds echo the ephemeral nature of beauty and the poignancy of isolation. The muted gray background and simple wooden surface enhance the flower’s warm glow, creating a harmonious contrast that invites both contemplation and a deep sense of serene introspection—making it an exquisite addition to any art collection.

artworks.title: The Christmas Tree
artworks.description: I painted this scene to capture the magical atmosphere of Vienna’s Rathaus during the Christmas season. The large tree with glowing heart-shaped lights represents warmth and human connection shining through the cold winter night. In the background, the Ferris wheel and small festive houses remind me of the joyful spirit of the city’s Christmas market. I chose vibrant reds, pinks, and deep blues to convey both the festive energy and the dreamlike feeling that fills Vienna at this time of year.

artworks.title: Under the Christmas Stars
artworks.description: I painted this piece to capture the quiet magic of a winter evening, when the world seems to glow from within. Surrounded by a sea of colorful paper stars, I felt as if time had slowed — a moment of peace and light in the midst of winter’s darkness. Each brushstroke holds that memory of warmth, reflection, and wonder.

artworks.title: Skeleton in the studio
artworks.description: Once, I was painting in my art school studio and saw a skeleton there. It intrigued me a lot, so the next time I brought my glasses and scarf, dressed it up, and tried to paint it. The artwork participated in several exhibitions and was chosen as the main piece for the next exhibition.

artworks.title: Paradise Apples
artworks.description: When I went to the studio to paint, I didn’t know what I would paint, but on the way I stopped by a flower shop and saw a wonderful branch with autumn paradise apples. This branch reflected my mood and desire to create the most—it was so delicate and at the same time ripe. A few apples fell off, I ate them and realized they were very tasty. They reminded me of my childhood because we used to pick these paradise apples in the botanical garden and make jam from them. While painting them, I remembered my mom, the warmth, autumn, and my home in Kyiv.

artworks.title: Clouds Over the Hills
artworks.description: This work was painted in May in the charming little town of Gaal. We stayed in a small van, but from it we had a wonderful view of the mountains. In the evenings, we could enjoy the atmosphere and watch the passing clouds. I tried to capture the very moment when the clouds and the sunlight meet.

artworks.title: Fanningberg 5
artworks.description:
One of six works created during my plein air session in Fanningberg Austria.

This painting was created on the last evening of my holiday in Fanningberg. The weather was beautiful, almost magical, and the light felt especially tender and quiet.

I was drawn to the glowing red rosehips against the cool winter surroundings. In that moment, everything felt still, yet alive. The snow reflected soft evening tones, and the branches seemed to hold the warmth of the day.

For me, this work carries the feeling of farewell — gratitude, calm, and a quiet inner glow at the end of something beautiful.

artworks.title: The Wave
artworks.description:
I painted this work on plein air in Turkey, Alanya standing right by the sea, immersed in the rhythm of the waves. My intention was to capture the breath of the water — that fleeting moment when a wave rises, gathers strength, and softly dissolves into foam.

This painting is about presence — a quiet dialogue between motion and stillness, where the sea seems to breathe.

When I look at this piece, I recall that breath of the sea, those waves, and the anticipation—how tall will it be, will it reach me? Alongside that, it holds the wonderful mood of painting en plein air with the other girls.

artworks.title: Where I Breathe More Quietly
artworks.description:
This place feels like a pause I deeply needed. When I painted it, I was listening to my own inner silence — calm, a little fragile, but warm. The river reflects not only the trees and the sky, but my own state of mind at that moment: restrained, thoughtful, and gently hopeful.
I softened the colors on purpose, letting cool blues and tender pinks blend without conflict, the way emotions do when they finally settle. For me, this landscape is about stillness without loneliness — about that quiet space where I can breathe more slowly and feel at peace with change that is coming, but not yet rushing me.

artworks.title: Cats patrol
artworks.description: I saw this picture 100 times. Cats are waiting for fish but intelligent and calm|) this charming coastal scene captures the peaceful bond between fishermen and the local cats, basking in the warmth of a quiet harbor. Where simplicity and nature come together in perfect harmony.

artworks.title: Spring in Blossom
artworks.description: Walking down the path, I was struck by a flowering tree and the magic of the landscape—the fresh greenery glittering around me and the abundance of blossoms all around. This original oil-on-canvas painting invites you into a sun‑dappled glade where delicate ivory and rose blooms adorn a tranquil woodland scene. Rendered in expressive, impressionistic brushstrokes, Spring in Full Bloom celebrates renewal, light, and nature’s fleeting beauty—perfect for adding warmth and poetic calm to any living space.

artworks.title: Quiet Morning in the Harbor
artworks.description: This oil painting depicts a peaceful harbor where fishing boats and yachts float on calm waters under a soft morning mist. The delicate reflections and gentle palette of blues and greys create a sense of serenity and connection to the sea. A work that brings quiet elegance and harmony to any space.

artworks.title: Never Againe
artworks.description: This oil painting captures one of the imposing flak towers located in Vienna’s historic Augarten park. Built during World War II as part of the city’s air defense system, the tower stands today as a powerful reminder of the past. The artist portrays the massive concrete structure against a soft blue sky, surrounded by autumnal trees in warm earthy tones. On the base of the tower, the words “Never Again” are inscribed — a poignant message of remembrance and hope for peace.

artworks.title: Evening Light at Cristmas
artworks.description: This painting depicts a tree near Vienna’s Rathaus during the Christmas season, surrounded by the glow of the famous Christmas markets. The warm festive lights blend with the cool twilight tones, creating a mysterious and poetic atmosphere. Together with the work “Christmas Tree”, it forms a harmonious pair — each painting complementing the other in mood and color, reflecting the quiet beauty and enchantment of winter in Vienna.

artworks.title: Stormlight Over the Mountain Path
artworks.description:
This painting captures the view from a mountain in Austria that I reached with the iconic Salamander train. Standing at the top, I was struck by the dramatic contrast between the gathering storm and the serene landscape below. A winding path leads through soft green hills, scattered rocks, and dark bushes toward the distant mountains, where heavy clouds sweep across the sky.

For a moment, rays of sunlight broke through the storm front, illuminating parts of the valley with a warm, almost magical glow. I tried to preserve this tension between light and shadow, calm and power, using expressive brushstrokes and natural, harmonious colors. This work reflects both the grandeur of Alpine nature and the sense of quiet awe I felt during that unforgettable ascent.

artworks.title: The Alpine Lake
artworks.description: We climbed uphill for a long time until we unexpectedly came across a small lake. Exhausted, we sat there, soaking in the silence and the mesmerizing play of autumn reflections on the water. A cozy wooden house rested on a sunlit hill, surrounded by lush pine trees, while its reflection danced on the glassy surface of the water. It was a deeply meditative and calming experience. I tried to capture that serene mood in this painting.

artworks.title: Life on a yacht
artworks.description:
This vibrant seascape captures the beauty of a peaceful Mediterranean harbor in Greece, Asos.  With sailboats gently floating and colorful houses tucked against the hills, the painting invites viewers into a serene coastal escape, filled with warmth and tranquility.
From personal experience life on a yachtbe different, but it is always an unforgettable experience.

artworks.title: Two Tails by the Lake
artworks.description: A Jack Russell Terrier and a fluffy white Terrier take a well-deserved break by a sunlit lake. These two aren’t just any dogs—they’re longtime companions of my friends, and capturing their unique charm and personality was key. Every detail was painted with care to make them instantly recognizable. A lighthearted scene full of tail wags, warm sun, and cherished memories.

artworks.title: Hydrangea Stillness
artworks.description: This delicate oil painting captures the timeless beauty of blooming hydrangeas in soft pink and lilac hues. Set against a natural linen canvas, the textured brushwork and lush green foliage bring a peaceful elegance to any space. Inspired by the quiet charm of nature, this still life is a celebration of grace, harmony, and fleeting springtime moments.

artworks.title: View from Mlini
artworks.description: Painted during a peaceful summer stay near Dubrovnik, View from Mlini captures the gentle charm of this small Croatian coastal town. I  depict the shimmering Adriatic Sea, dotted with sailboats, as seen from the terrace of a seaside apartment. Lush green hills frame the harbor, while warm sunlight dances across the rooftops and water. This evocative oil painting invites the viewer to pause and take in the slow rhythm of Mediterranean life — a perfect piece for those who treasure travel, light, and coastal serenity.

artworks.title: Track #4
artworks.description:
Thpainting was created from life and depicts Track No. 4, a quiet winter ski slope leading through snow-covered terrain toward distant mountains. A wide, open sky and soft winter light create a strong sense of space and calm.

Painted directly on site, the work focuses on atmosphere rather than detail, using expressive brushstrokes to convey cold air, movement, and the clarity of a winter day.

artworks.title: Red Amaryllis
artworks.description:
This gouache painting was created from life in Rovinj, Croatia, during I stay at a hotel of the Maistra Hospitality Group. Painted on the last day before Christmas, the work carries a personal and intimate atmosphere shaped by travel, winter stillness, and a temporary studio space away from home.

The blooming red amaryllis becomes a symbol of warmth and quiet intensity, standing in contrast to the cooler tones of the surrounding space. Expressive brushstrokes and vibrant color reflect direct observation and an emotional connection to the moment — the calm of a hotel interior, the pause before the holiday, and the presence of the living.

artworks.title: Before the Next Wave
artworks.description:
I painted this work from life during a plein air session by the sea in Turkey. The moment was calm yet filled with quiet anticipation, as the waves rolled gently toward the shore. I was drawn to the subtle shifts of color in the water and the soft atmosphere that blurred the distance between sea, land, and sky. A lone figure walking along the beach adds a sense of scale and solitude, capturing the peaceful rhythm of the coast.
Alongside that, it holds the wonderful mood of painting en plein air with the other girls.

artworks.title: Shoes
artworks.description: I bought these shoes a long time ago from an online store. I really liked them for their color and shape. But unfortunately, the width, and even the size, turned out not to be mine. But I love them so much, I love looking at them, and I dream that one day, maybe my feet will slim down a bit, and I'll be able to wear them.  They have a wonderful eggplant color. They symbolize the desire to look beautiful.

artworks.title: Juicy summer
artworks.description: Juicy summer is the beginning when watermelons start to be sold, and the bright colors of red and ultramarine simply enchant you. This vibrant still-life features a deep blue bottle, a juicy watermelon slice, and an array of red berries and cherries. The bold colors against the dark background create a striking contrast, making this piece a captivating celebration of color and texture.

artworks.title: Ukrainian doll
artworks.description: I decided to depict the ukrainian doll and the vase, which was also made in Ukraine, as a reminder and a bit of nostalgia, an autumnal longing for my homeland. This doll was gifted to me and made with my husband’s sister, and the vase was brought from Ukraine. Together, they complement each other. The red berries also grow in Ukraine, but I bought them in Austria, and I think they look very picturesque in the gray vase.

artworks.title: The Heritage
artworks.description:
This painting is a heartfelt tribute to my heritage, capturing cherished memories through personal items. It features photographs of my grandparents, alongside a 100-karbovanets note, known as the ‘Yekaterynenka,’ symbolizing the savings started by my husband’s grandparents. The painting also includes my husband’s vintage camera and his grandfather’s handmade embroidered shirt, lovingly crafted by his grandmother. Each element tells a story of our roots, connections, and shared memories, bringing together past generations in one artwork.
I did with grate love and gratefullness.

artworks.title: The whispering Emotions
artworks.description: This artwork was inspired by my journey to Étretat, France—a place where nature and art merge in harmony. The vivid green hues of the park, contrasting with the gray masks representing human emotions, captivated me. Each mask symbolizes a different feeling, blending with the natural landscape as if emotions were part of the earth itself.

artworks.title: Two kohlrabi
artworks.description:
This oil painting presents a vibrant still life of fresh kohlrabi, gracefully arranged on a white surface against a deep burgundy backdrop. The rich green leaves extend across the canvas, contrasting beautifully with the delicate, round bulbs that cast soft shadows. The interplay of light and color gives the composition depth and warmth, making the simple vegetable appear almost sculptural.

This artwork is dedicated to my dear friend, who introduced me to the joy of eating raw kohlrabi as a snack. It’s incredibly delicious, and this painting captures not only the beauty of this humble vegetable but also the warm memories of shared moments and new discoveries.

artworks.title: Cactus Serenade
artworks.description: While walking along a path to the sea on Hvar Island, Croatia, I encountered remarkable cacti that appeared like living beings stretching their arms toward me. The light filtering through them was mesmerizing, compelling me to grab my painting supplies. This artwork, filled with warmth and light, evokes pleasant positive emotions, capturing the beauty and tranquility of that moment.

artworks.title: Gift of the Moment
artworks.description: A spontaneous and heartfelt painting inspired by an unexpected yet delightful gift from a dear friend. The vibrant pink roses, elegantly arranged in a modern vase, radiate warmth and appreciation. Soft shadows and delicate details capture the fleeting beauty of the moment, preserving the emotions of gratitude and joy on canvas. A timeless reminder that the best gifts come from the heart.

artworks.title: Asparagus on Wild Garlic
artworks.description:
The painting features a fresh bunch of asparagus, tied with a purple ribbon, resting on vibrant wild garlic leaves. The background is rendered in contrasting tones: a deep burgundy at the top gradually transitions into a light beige at the bottom. The meticulous detailing of the asparagus and wild garlic creates a sense of naturalness and freshness. This still life highlights the simplicity and authenticity of the subject, conveying a feeling of coziness and harmony through the combination of natural colors.

The artwork was inspired by Édouard Manet, whose unique approach to still life and attention to everyday objects served as a source of creative inspiration.

artworks.title: Where the Water Waits
artworks.description:
Painted en plein air on the banks of the Danube in Vienna during the summer of 2024, this piece captures a quiet, atmospheric moment at sunset. A modern riverside residence blends into lush greenery, its reflection resting softly on the water’s surface.

The gentle evening light, cool tones, and peaceful setting evoke a sense of calm and introspection. Created directly from life, this work carries the immediacy of the moment — the warmth of summer air, the stillness of the river, and the subtle sounds of nature surrounding the artist.

This painting is a visual memory — a dialogue between architecture and nature, between movement and stillness.

artworks.title: A Mother's Watch
artworks.description: Created during a short vacation in May, this painting was inspired  me by the profound sight of a mother sheep constantly worrying about and protecting her young lamb. The artist sought to capture that tender moment of watchfulness. Bathed in the soft light of a sun-dappled meadow, the scene radiates a sense of peace, innocence, and the powerful, anxious love of a mother for her child.

artworks.title: Bottles at Dusk
artworks.description: Painted in gouache during a harbor dinner — but I couldn’t touch the food until I captured this beauty on paper. A still life of fishing tools, rusty chains, and colorful glass bottles, transformed by light into a quiet story of the sea.

artworks.title: Drift in Grey Light
artworks.description: Painted in gouache directly from the deck of a catamaran, this plein air work captures the quiet beauty of a cloudy morning on the Croatian coast. A personal memory of travel, nature, and the sea. It was my first experience spending the night on a catamaran in pouring rain, without being able to go ashore or enjoy the sun at all.

artworks.title: Bay Sketch before the Storm
artworks.description: This is a quick plein air gouache sketch, painted directly from life on board a catamaran during an overnight stay in a secluded bay. That night, a sudden rainstorm caught us by surprise. The finished work even fell into the salty water — yet it dried without any damage, and the colors now seem even brighter and more vivid. The painting captures not only the scenery but also the atmosphere of spontaneity and adventure, making it a unique piece with its own story.

artworks.title: Evening over Gaal
artworks.description: Painted en plein air in the mountains about two hours from Vienna, this oil painting captures the tranquil beauty of the Gaal region. The rolling hills are shown in deep green tones, contrasting with a luminous evening sky filled with soft clouds touched by pink and silver light. The work conveys both freshness of direct observation and a meditative calm, reflecting the harmony of nature.

artworks.title: Pines
artworks.description:
I painted this gouache from life in Croatia, standing among the pine trees and working directly in the warm Mediterranean light. I was drawn to the rhythm of the trunks, the soft movement of the branches, and the quiet space between the trees.

This work is less about detail and more about a feeling — warmth, air, and stillness — a brief moment of calm experienced and painted on site.

artworks.title: Sea light and silence
artworks.description:
I painted this piece from life during a plein air session in Alanya, Turkey.
I was drawn to the quiet dialogue between the cliffs and the sea, where soft waves gently touched the rocky shore. The morning light muted the colors into delicate blues and greens, creating a peaceful, almost dreamlike atmosphere. Working directly from nature, I tried to capture not only the shapes of the landscape, but also the feeling of  fresh air, and the subtle rhythm of the water.

Alongside that, it holds the wonderful mood of painting en plein air with the other girls.

artworks.title: Echo of the Shore
artworks.description:
I painted this piece en plein air on the coast, drawn by the quiet, almost weightless atmosphere of the place. 

I have returned to these rocks many times, painting them again and again in different moods and light. Each time they felt different — sometimes calm, sometimes more dramatic, sometimes almost dissolving into the air. In this work, they appeared soft and quiet, as if the landscape itself was resting.

I was interested in capturing not the details, but the feeling — that moment when everything slows down, and the boundary between water, air, and land becomes almost invisible.

Alongside that, it holds the wonderful mood of painting en plein air with the other girls.

artworks.title: Stone Colors
artworks.description:
I painted this piece en plein air in Turkey, inspired by the clear, sunlit water and the stones beneath the surface. I loved how the light reveals their soft shapes and colors, almost like hidden little worlds.

For me, this painting is about calmness — the quiet feeling of looking into the water, where everything slows down and becomes simple and clear. And I was searching for the colors of the stones—they turned out to be so varied, with every spectrum. It was fascinating to see such a rich range hidden there

artworks.title: Cosmos Flowers and Humor
artworks.description: This  still life painting combines classic elements with a playful twist. Richly textured cosmos flowers in a ceramic beers jug, bright seasonal gourds, and a lively draped background form the composition, while a quirky rubber chicken adds unexpected humor. Perfect for adding both elegance and whimsy to any space.

artworks.title: The Twists of History
artworks.description:
A book about heroic times that repeat through history sets the tone for this evocative still-life. A bull mask, a historical portrait, and a bowl of ash come together to symbolize tradition, memory, and the enduring cycles of human experience.
This artwork is an excellent choice for a study or office space.

artworks.title: Spring Dance of the Tree
artworks.description: This oil painting depicts a graceful tree swaying in the gentle embrace of spring. Its twisted trunk and sprawling branches create a dynamic, almost dance-like movement, reflected in the calm waters of a winding canal. The vibrant blue sky and warm sunlight bring a sense of renewal and serenity, making this artwork a perfect addition to any space that celebrates nature’s beauty.

artworks.title: Red Beets on Linen
artworks.description:
This striking still life is part of the artist’s ongoing series “Fruits and Vegetables on Transparent Linen.” It captures the raw beauty of freshly harvested red beets, their vibrant crimson stems and textured green leaves arranged with natural elegance. The composition is painted directly onto transparent linen, allowing the fabric’s texture and tone to become an integral part of the artwork. This unique approach adds warmth, depth, and a contemporary feel to a classical subject.

The rich indigo background contrasts beautifully with the earthy tones of the vegetables, while the natural linen surface enhances the organic atmosphere of the painting. Light and shadow are used masterfully to create a serene, contemplative mood.
A refined piece that brings both rustic charm and artistic sophistication to any space.

artworks.title: In the Light and Shadow
artworks.description: These bright birthday roses now live on as a memory, captured in oil on canvas. Sunlight dances across the table, casting deep shadows that tell their own story. Hidden gently in the shade, a beloved cat watches quietly—a part of the home, always present. This painting is about warmth, stillness, and the simple beauty of everyday moments shared with those we love.

artworks.title: Chicken tea party
artworks.description: This work is a still life. It depicts an old teapot and a plate of pies. I baked the pies myself, and their smell inspired me to create this piece. On top, there is a dog toy – a chicken. I really liked its shape. And it might symbolize something rural and playful, connected to nature.

artworks.title: Blossoms of Hope
artworks.description: This work was created in winter, inspired by the anticipation of spring and the desire for everything to be beautiful and blooming. Featuring delicate pink flowers on bold turquoise tones, I want to  capture a sense of renewal, harmony, and the promise of warmer days.

artworks.title: At the erdge of the world
artworks.description: A vibrant beach scene painted in Portugal during a summer holiday. Colorful umbrellas, sunbathers, and boats on a calm blue sea capture the warmth and charm of coastal life.  It’s perfect for adding a touch of summer to any space.

artworks.title: Waiting for the Cristmas
artworks.description:
A vibrant red amaryllis, the flower of Christmas and hope, stands tall against a cloudy sky with glimpses of light breaking through. The festive still life, with evergreen branches, ornaments, and a reflective glass vase, was created in the studio on a gloomy day just before Christmas, bringing warmth and light to the season.

Unfortunately, the work was stolen and is currently being sought by its owner.

artworks.title: Before the Storm
artworks.description:
Before the Storm” was born from a quiet yet unforgettable moment during a walk through the historic park of Laxenburg, near Vienna, Austria. As dark clouds began gathering above, I looked for shelter near a small bridge — but couldn’t resist stopping. The golden light pierced through the sky, dancing on the treetops and the water, while the warm air carried the scent of summer and the soft hum of insects.

That fleeting contrast — the sunlight fighting through the coming storm, the heavy air, and the vibrant life around — is something I still feel vividly. I tried to preserve that atmosphere in this painting: the calm before the change, nature holding its breath.

This original oil painting invites you to experience that same pause — a moment of connection, reflection, and awe.

artworks.title: Sunlit Amaryllis
artworks.description:
While I was painting the flower, someone was painting me — and in that moment I felt beautifully exposed and alive. Then, almost magically, the sun burst into the room and defeated the studio light in just one minute. A deep, dramatic shadow appeared, and it touched me so strongly that I painted it without thinking.

This artwork carries that moment exactly as I lived it: the flower, the sunlight, the shadow, and the feeling of being seen — gently, unexpectedly, and truly.
